notes.content: The Art History major combines an interdisciplinary and intercultural approach to art history of all periods and places. By thinking across current categories and boundaries and critically interrogating art history itself, students are encouraged to question the canon, to rethink the relationships between margins and centers, and to practice a socially and politically responsible art history. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, please complete two or more courses listed below by the end of spring before transfer: Ancient art Medieval art Renaissance and Baroque art Modern art Museum studies Indian/Southeast Asian art African art Chinese art Japanese art Pre-Columbian art It is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED to complete additional major preparatory courses. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://arthistory.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation courses for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
notes.contentHtml:
The Art History major combines an interdisciplinary and intercultural approach to art history of all periods and places. By thinking across current categories and boundaries and critically interrogating art history itself, students are encouraged to question the canon, to rethink the relationships between margins and centers, and to practice a socially and politically responsible art history.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major.


To be considered for this major, please complete two or more courses listed below by the end of spring before transfer:

Ancient art

Medieval art

Renaissance and Baroque art

Modern art

Museum studies

Indian/Southeast Asian art

African art

Chinese art

Japanese art

Pre-Columbian art


It is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED to complete additional major preparatory courses.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://arthistory.ucla.edu and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation courses for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

notes.content: While the Art history department does not require language beyond the College requirement, Art History majors are encouraged to study foreign language beyond what is required by the College. Students should save syllabi for all art history and art courses to petition the department for exemption from lower division requirements after admission.
notes.contentHtml: While the Art history department does not require language beyond the College requirement, Art History majors are encouraged to study foreign language beyond what is required by the College. Students should save syllabi for all art history and art courses to petition the department for exemption from lower division requirements after admission.

notes.content: No AP or IB art history exams are considered equivalent, and credit earned from those exams cannot be used towards the Art History major prep.
notes.contentHtml: No AP or IB art history exams are considered equivalent, and credit earned from those exams cannot be used towards the Art History major prep.
